question:
What will be the output of the following code?

```python
for i in range(5):
    if i == 2:
        continue
    print(i)
```
response:
I think the "continue" statement means that the loop will stop when it reaches 2, but I'm not really sure what it does to the rest of the loop. Maybe it just skips the rest of the code inside the loop when i is 2? 

My answer is: B. I think this is right because the loop will print all the numbers except 2, since the "continue" statement makes it skip the print statement when i is 2.
